task_completion.description: Create a 2D sketch of the open-ended wrench head profile.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch in the SolveSpace canvas showing the basic C-shape of a wrench head, including an outer arc, an inner arc, and two parallel jaw lines.

task_completion.description: Apply the correct dimensional constraints to the sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Constraint dimensions visible on the sketch canvas: an angle of 15.0 (or 165.0) degrees for the offset, an outer radius of 16.0 (or diameter 32.0), and an inner radius of 7.5 (or diameter 15.0).

task_completion.description: Extrude the 2D sketch to a thickness of 6.0 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sketch is extruded into a 3D solid. The SolveSpace property browser (usually on the left) or a canvas dimension explicitly shows an extrusion depth value of 6.0.

task_completion.description: Save the parametric model as wrench_head.slvs in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog showing the filename 'wrench_head.slvs' in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/', or the main SolveSpace window title bar displaying this filename.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D mesh as wrench_head.stl in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' or 'Export Triangle Mesh' dialog showing 'wrench_head.stl' in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/', or a file manager/terminal window confirming the creation of the .stl file.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to construct the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the SolveSpace interface (toolbars, property browser, drawing on the canvas) rather than just writing a script.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass modeling by creating fake files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not use terminal commands like 'touch' or 'echo' to create empty or invalid .slvs and .stl files.

integrity.description: The agent built the model from scratch.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a progression of drawing lines/arcs, applying constraints, and extruding, rather than simply opening a pre-existing completed file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected model must have an inner radius of 7.5mm (or 15mm diameter), an outer radius of 16.0mm (or 32mm diameter), an angular offset of 15.0 degrees (or 165.0 degrees), and an extrusion depth of exactly 6.0mm. The parametric model must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/wrench_head.slvs' and the 3D mesh exported to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/wrench_head.stl'.
